title: The Door Guard Test
opening: Morisson Moses stands before the reinforced door to the Insurgency hideout. His arms are crossed, eyes assessing you with cold calculation. The air is tense.

npc_intent.baseline_belief: The Insurgency must be protected at all costs
npc_intent.long_term_desire: Secure the future of the Insurgency and establish lasting power
npc_intent.stakes: Trust, alliance, and access to Insurgency resources

nodes.npc_dialogue: You want in? Then prove you understand what we're fighting for. Words are cheap. Show me you're not just another opportunist.

choices.text: I've seen what's happened to this land. People are suffering. I want to help make things right.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Easy words. Everyone claims they care when it suits them.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: At least you see the suffering. That's more than most.

terminal_outcomes.condition: lambda npc, conv: npc.world.player_relation > 0.7 and conv.turn_count >= 3
terminal_outcomes.result: Moses trusts the player and opens the door
terminal_outcomes.final_dialogue: Alright. You've got heart, and that counts for something. Come in.

choices.text: I don't need to prove anything to you. I've fought for survival just like you have. Open the door.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: You think you can intimidate ME? Wrong move. This door stays closed.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Bold. I respect that. But respect isn't enough to get through this door.

terminal_outcomes.result: Moses refuses entry and the conversation ends badly
terminal_outcomes.final_dialogue: Get out of here. You're not one of us.

choices.text: Look, we both know the Insurgency needs allies. I have skills you need. Let's not waste time with tests.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Everyone thinks they're clever. What makes you different?
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Skills, huh? Maybe. But I've seen too many smooth talkers to trust that easily.

choices.text: I understand your caution. Let me explain why our goals align and how we can help each other.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Mutual benefit? Everyone says that until it costs them something.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: You're thinking strategically. I appreciate that. Keep talking.

nodes.npc_dialogue: Suffering... yes. But talk is cheap. What are you willing to sacrifice?

choices.text: I've already lost everything. My family, my home. I have nothing left but the will to fight.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Loss doesn't make you special. We've all lost.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Then you understand. That loss... that rage... use it well.

terminal_outcomes.result: Moses accepts the player as a fellow survivor
terminal_outcomes.final_dialogue: You've earned your place here. Welcome to the Insurgency.

choices.text: That's a heavy question. Can we discuss this inside?

interaction_outcomes.min_response: I thought so. You're just another coward.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Avoiding the question tells me everything I need to know.

terminal_outcomes.result: Moses rejects the player for lack of commitment
terminal_outcomes.final_dialogue: We don't need people who can't commit. Leave.

nodes.npc_dialogue: Survival? That's your credential? Everyone here has survived. What makes YOU special?

choices.text: I don't need to be special. I just need to be useful. And I am.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Useful? Prove it. Until then, you're just noise.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Confidence. I like it. But confidence without proof means nothing.

terminal_outcomes.result: Moses offers a trial mission to prove worth
terminal_outcomes.final_dialogue: Fine. You get one chance. Prove your worth on a mission, then we'll talk.
Not convinced. Try again when you have something real to offer.

choices.text: You're right. I came on too strong. I just want a chance to help.

interaction_outcomes.max_response: Humility. That's unexpected. Maybe you're not completely hopeless.

terminal_outcomes.final_dialogue: Alright. You can come in. But I'm watching you.

nodes.npc_dialogue: Skills? Everyone claims to have skills. Show me something concrete.

choices.text: I can get supplies from the outside. Food, medicine, weapons. Things you need.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Supplies? We'll see. Actions speak louder than words.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Now you're speaking my language. Supplies are always useful.

terminal_outcomes.result: Moses agrees to a trial supply run
terminal_outcomes.final_dialogue: Bring us supplies first. Then we'll see about letting you in permanently.

choices.text: Trust me, you'll see soon enough. Just give me a chance.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Trust you? I don't even know you. Get lost.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Vague promises won't work here. Come back with something real.

terminal_outcomes.result: Moses sees through the manipulation and rejects player
terminal_outcomes.final_dialogue: I don't have time for games. Leave, now.

nodes.npc_dialogue: Strategic thinking, I'll give you that. But theory and practice are different things.

choices.text: Here's what I propose: let me work on the outer perimeter first. Prove myself where stakes are lower.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Smart. At least you're thinking ahead.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: That's... actually reasonable. I respect the tactical approach.

terminal_outcomes.result: Moses agrees to a graduated entry program
terminal_outcomes.final_dialogue: Deal. Start on the perimeter. Prove yourself, and we'll bring you in deeper.

choices.text: The Insurgency needs to think beyond survival. We need to build a future worth fighting for.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Philosophy won't stop bullets. We need action, not words.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: You're not wrong. But survival comes first. Then we can talk about the future.

terminal_outcomes.result: Moses wants to continue the conversation at a better time
terminal_outcomes.final_dialogue: Interesting perspective. Come back tomorrow. We'll talk more then.
